evals.prompt: We need a content plan for the 6 weeks leading up to launch. We're Sproutly, a small-business email-marketing tool for local e-commerce shops. Help us build an audience and drive launch signups.
evals.expected_output: An objective and funnel map, three to five content pillars tied to the message pillars, and a concrete dated 6-week calendar where each slot names date, channel, format, working title/hook, pillar, and funnel stage. Effort is balanced across funnel stages and at least one growth loop is included. Program leans high-velocity SEO/social/self-serve per the small-business segment. Saved to .vital/marketing.md.
assertions: Output states the objective and maps it to funnel stages (awareness, consideration, activation, retention) with effort balanced rather than all-awareness
Three to five content pillars are defined and tied to the message pillars
The calendar is concrete and dated: every slot lists date, channel, format, working title/hook, pillar, and funnel stage (not a themes-by-week abstraction)
The calendar covers the full requested 6-week pre-launch window
At least one growth loop where output feeds input (referral, UGC, content-to-audience, or product-generated) is identified
Formats and channels lean to the small-business segment (high-velocity SEO, social, self-serve education) rather than enterprise whitepapers/webinars

evals.prompt: Design a launch email sequence and an SEO cluster strategy for Finhorizon, an enterprise financial-planning platform sold to CFOs at large enterprises. ICP and messaging are saved in the project.
evals.expected_output: Pulls ICP and messaging from project, defines the objective and funnel map, delivers lifecycle email flows with trigger/sequence/per-message goal and drafted critical emails in the brand voice, and an SEO strategy with topic clusters, a pillar page and supporting pages per cluster, and priority order by intent and winnability. Every program is tied to a metric. Enterprise-weighted formats (thought leadership, whitepapers, ABM/nurture).
assertions: Email flows specify the trigger, the full sequence, the goal of each message, and include drafts of the critical emails in the brand voice
Email subject lines and body copy are drafted (not just described) for the key emails
SEO output identifies topic clusters with a pillar page and supporting pages per cluster and a priority order by intent and winnability
Each program is mapped to a funnel stage and names the metric it moves
Content approach is weighted to the enterprise segment (thought leadership, whitepapers, longer nurture, account-based) rather than high-velocity self-serve
SEO clusters are anchored to real ICP search behavior rather than vanity keywords

evals.prompt: I just want a few good LinkedIn posts for next week to promote our new integration. We're Meshwork, a mid-market API-integration platform. Keep it light.
evals.expected_output: Honors the narrow ask but applies the engine discipline: states the objective and the funnel stage the posts serve, ties them to a content pillar, produces a small dated calendar of concrete posts with working hooks, drafts the post copy in brand voice (checking for a saved my-writing-style profile), and names the metric. Mid-market comparison/ROI-flavored angle. Does not balloon into a full multi-channel program uninvited.
assertions: Output states the objective and which funnel stage the posts serve rather than treating them as one-off content
Each post is tied to a content pillar and the program names the metric it moves
Posts are delivered as a concrete dated set with working titles/hooks and drafted copy, not abstract themes
The skill checks for a saved my-writing-style profile before drafting copy the client will publish as themselves
Angle and framing fit the mid-market segment (e.g. comparison or ROI emphasis) rather than enterprise whitepaper tone
Scope stays proportional to the light request rather than expanding into a full multi-channel calendar unprompted
